subject: Cloud Computing
title: Monolith vs. Distributed Systems
description: A quick win: understand Monolith vs. Distributed Systems.

hook.text: A single application handles everything: users, payments, emails. One bug crashes it all. That's a monolith.

buildup.text: Distributed systems split work across many services. Each runs independently and communicates over the network.

discovery.text: Monoliths are simple to build and deploy. Distributed systems are flexible but far more complex to manage.

twist.text: Many teams split monoliths too early. The complexity of distributed systems outweighs the benefits at small scale.

climax.text: The best approach: start monolith, split later when specific pain points emerge. Don't distribute for its own sake.

punchline.text: Monoliths aren't bad. Premature distribution is worse.

quiz.question: When should a team typically move from a monolith to distributed services?
options: When specific pain points emerge that distribution would solve
As soon as the first line of code is written
Only after the company has more than 1,000 employees
